page: Amazon Web Services r5n.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Cache resources consist of a 32 KB L1d cache, a 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it features an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates that the r5n.2xlarge delivers average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth. In stress-ng multi-core testing and Geekbench multi-core evaluations, the instance scores in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also fall within the average range, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at 52.94 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 40.35 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression scoring in the bottom 25% tier. Large language model inference speeds are also limited, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model categorized in the bottom 10% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5n.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance single-core compute. The presence of hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES supports cryptographic and vector operations. Given the average performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, this instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database caching, and general-purpose memory-optimized workloads rather than compute-heavy or storage-intensive tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and compression tasks fall into the bottom 25% tier. Network throughput is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database operations where memory capacity is prioritized over raw single-core compute speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized instance with 64 GB RAM, running on an Intel Xeon processor with 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and balanced multi-core performance for memory-bound workloads.

Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz
